questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Aromatic amines are more basic than aliphatic amines.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Aliphatic amines have a weaker inductive effect compared to aromatic amines, making aromatic amines more basic.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is because aromatic amines have a stronger electron-donating effect due to the resonance of the lone pair on the nitrogen atom, making them more basic.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Aliphatic amines are less stable than aromatic amines.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Aliphatic amines have a weaker inductive effect compared to aromatic amines.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is not true as aliphatic amines are generally more stable than aromatic amines due to the absence of resonance stabilization in aliphatic amines.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Aliphatic amines are less basic than aromatic amines.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Primary amines are more basic than secondary amines.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Primary amines have a weaker steric effect compared to secondary amines.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is because primary amines have a less hindered nitrogen atom, making it more accessible for protonation, hence more basic.
